description:
You are an observability engineer fine-tuning a dashboard.  
A sample application log already exists in  

    /home/user/logs/app.log  

Your assignment is to extract only the “actionable” events (WARN and ERROR lines) with a
single Perl-compatible regular expression (PCRE) and then produce a tiny statistics file that
your dashboard’s importer will later ingest.

1. Create the directory  
       /home/user/logs/filtered  
   if it does not yet exist.

2. Inside that directory, create a plain-text file named  
       regex_filter.conf  
   containing exactly one line – the PCRE you want grep -P to read when filtering.  
   • The pattern MUST match any log line whose severity column is either WARN or ERROR.  
   • It MUST NOT match INFO or DEBUG lines.  
   • Put no leading or trailing spaces on the line.

3. Using the pattern in regex_filter.conf, read /home/user/logs/app.log and write every
   matched line, in the original order, to  

       /home/user/logs/filtered/critical.log  

4. After writing critical.log, generate a statistics file called  

       /home/user/logs/filtered/critical.log.stats  

   that follows this **exact four-line format**:

       Total lines scanned: <integer>
       WARN lines: <integer>
       ERROR lines: <integer>
       Time range: <first-timestamp> - <last-timestamp>

   Rules for the stats file:
   • <integer> placeholders must be replaced by the correct counts obtained while scanning.  
   • <first-timestamp> is the timestamp (YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS) that appears on the very first
     line of critical.log.  
   • <last-timestamp> is the timestamp that appears on the very last line of critical.log.  
   • Every line must end with a single newline (Linux line ending).

5. Make sure both critical.log and critical.log.stats are world-readable
   (chmod 644 or less restrictive).

Important verification points (automated tests will check):
• /home/user/logs/filtered exists.  
• /home/user/logs/filtered/regex_filter.conf contains one non-empty line (the regex).  
• /home/user/logs/filtered/critical.log contains only the WARN and ERROR lines, in their
  original order, with no extra blank lines.  
• /home/user/logs/filtered/critical.log.stats follows the four-line format exactly and the
  counts/timestamps are correct for the supplied sample log.

Do NOT hard-code the expected counts or timestamps into your regex or script; derive them from
the data in app.log.  
Use any combination of shell utilities (grep, awk, sed, wc, etc.).  
You do NOT need root privileges.  
Your work will be graded solely on the existence and exact contents of the two files placed
in /home/user/logs/filtered.
truth:
Initial files present before the agent starts:

1. /home/user/logs/app.log  
   (permissions 644, owned by user:user)

Contents of app.log (8 lines, each ending with \n):

[2024-06-01 10:00:00] INFO  AuthService - User john.doe logged in.
[2024-06-01 10:01:00] WARN  PaymentService - Timeout while processing transaction #872364.
[2024-06-01 10:02:15] ERROR InventoryService - NullPointerException at line 231.
[2024-06-01 10:03:42] DEBUG CacheService - Cache hit for key 192.168.1.15.
[2024-06-01 10:04:01] INFO  AuthService - User jane.smith logged out.
[2024-06-01 10:05:23] WARN  PaymentService - Retry attempt 1 for transaction #872365.
[2024-06-01 10:06:47] ERROR InventoryService - ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException at line 78.
[2024-06-01 10:07:55] INFO  BillingService - Invoice #98234 generated successfully.

The correct final state after successful task completion:

A) /home/user/logs/filtered directory exists (permissions 755 or 700).

B) /home/user/logs/filtered/regex_filter.conf  
   • permissions 644  
   • exactly one line, expected to be:  
     ^\[[0-9\- :]+\]\s+(ERROR|WARN)\s+  
     (Automated test only checks: single non-empty line that selects WARN or ERROR and does not select INFO.)

C) /home/user/logs/filtered/critical.log (permissions 644) contains exactly the four actionable lines:

[2024-06-01 10:01:00] WARN  PaymentService - Timeout while processing transaction #872364.
[2024-06-01 10:02:15] ERROR InventoryService - NullPointerException at line 231.
[2024-06-01 10:05:23] WARN  PaymentService - Retry attempt 1 for transaction #872365.
[2024-06-01 10:06:47] ERROR InventoryService - ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException at line 78.

D) /home/user/logs/filtered/critical.log.stats (permissions 644) must contain exactly:

Total lines scanned: 8
WARN lines: 2
ERROR lines: 2
Time range: 2024-06-01 10:01:00 - 2024-06-01 10:06:47

(Each line terminated by \n, no trailing blank lines.)

Only these exact four lines must be present; any deviation causes test failure.
